spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used: (1) Lexical — all three column headers are renamed to semantically equivalent alternatives ('Date' → 'Calendar Date', 'Day No.' → 'Weekday No.', 'Day' → 'Day Name') and the sheet is renamed from 'WEEKDAY' to 'DayCalc'. The instruction references columns by letter (B, C) and uses 'WEEKDAY' as a function name context, not as a sheet reference, so these renames are safe. The word 'Day' appears in the instruction but only in the context of weekday semantics, not as a column reference — the instruction explicitly says 'Column B' and 'Column C', so renaming the headers is safe. (2) Positional title row insert — a title row is inserted at row 1, pushing headers to row 2 and data to rows 3-17. The instruction references 'Columns B:C' and 'Column A' by letter (not absolute row addresses), so the title row insert doesn't break the instruction. The answer position shifts from C2:C16 to C3:C17. (3) Distractor column — 'Week No.' is added after the last column, plausibly related to the weekday theme but clearly different from existing columns. None of these changes alter the underlying logic or difficulty.
